source_subject: mmc: test A733 CMD18 PIO up to 256 blocks for diag
bootargs: console=ttyS0,115200n8 earlycon=uart8250,mmio32,0x02500000 loglevel=8 ignore_loglevel drm_debug=1 root=PARTUUID=db375e07-7682-4d4e-b8bc-a923dd0b027e rootfstype=ext4 rootwait ro rootflags=noload init=/bin/sh
pass_evidence: EXT4-fs (mmcblk0p3): mounted filesystem ... ro without journal at 75.061663s
VFS: Mounted root (ext4 filesystem) readonly on device 179:3 at 75.073074s
VFS: Pivoted into new rootfs at 86.905442s
boundary_evidence: PIO CMD18 reads completed up to 256 blocks and 131072-byte bcntr paths.
Later CMD25 write used normal IDMA: opcode=25, blocks=16, wait_dma=1.
CMD25 stalled with IDST=0x00004000, CBCR=0, BBCR=0, and descriptor OWN still set.
conclusion: H086 is a useful lab-only read/runtime path and not an upstream fix. The real blocker remains A733 SDMMC0 normal IDMAC descriptor-fetch reachability.
